title: Student Loan Forgiveness Hides a $21,000 Tax Bomb
hook: After 25 years of income-based payments, millions of borrowers get their loans 'forgiven' — then the IRS sends a bill for $21,000.
angle: The phantom income tax bomb hidden inside income-driven repayment forgiveness that 8.8 million borrowers never see coming
audience: Student loan borrowers on income-driven repayment, millennials and Gen Z with grad school debt aged 25-50
key_numbers: average grad borrower has $95,000+ forgiven after 25 years
$21,000 federal tax bill at 22% bracket on $95,000 forgiven
forgiven balance taxed as ordinary income via Form 1099-C
PSLF forgiveness is the only tax-free alternative
